name: vadersentiment

metadata.name: vaderSentiment
metadata.summary: VADER Sentiment Analysis. VADER (Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner) is a lexicon and rule-based sentiment analysis tool that is specifically attuned to sentiments expressed in social media, and works well on texts from other domains.

documentation:
VADER (Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner) is a lexicon and rule-based sentiment analysis tool that is *specifically attuned to sentiments expressed in social media*. It is fully open-sourced under the `[MIT License] <http://choosealicense.com/>`_ (we sincerely appreciate all attributions and readily accept most contributions, but please don't hold us liable).
        
        **Citation Information**
        
        If you use either the dataset or any of the VADER sentiment analysis tools (VADER sentiment lexicon or Python code for rule-based sentiment analysis engine) in your research, please cite the above paper. For example:  
        
          **Hutto, C.J. & Gilbert, E.E. (2014). VADER: A Parsimonious Rule-based Model for Sentiment Analysis of Social Media Text. Eighth International Conference on Weblogs and Social Media (ICWSM-14). Ann Arbor, MI, June 2014.** 
        
        
        * Code examples are provided on the `[VADER GitHub Repo] <https://github.com/cjhutto/vaderSentiment>`_
        * Details about the scoring are provided on the `[VADER GitHub Repo] <https://github.com/cjhutto/vaderSentiment>`_
        * VADER has been ported to Java, JavaScript, PHP, Scala, C#, Rust, and Go (see details and links to these on the `[VADER GitHub Repo] <https://github.com/cjhutto/vaderSentiment>`_
        
        
        
Keywords: vader,sentiment,analysis,opinion,mining,nlp,text,data,text analysis,opinion analysis,sentiment analysis,text mining,twitter sentiment,opinion mining,social media,twitter,social,media
Platform: any
Classifier: Development Status :: 5 - Production/Stable
Classifier: Intended Audience :: Science/Research
Classifier: License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License
Classifier: Natural Language :: English
Classifier: Programming Language :: Python :: 3.5
Classifier: Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Artificial Intelligence
Classifier: Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Information Analysis
Classifier: Topic :: Text Processing :: Linguistic
Classifier: Topic :: Text Processing :: General
Description-Content-Type: text/x-rst

modules.name: vaderSentiment
documentation: !/usr/bin/python

modules.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment
documentation:
If you use the VADER sentiment analysis tools, please cite:
Hutto, C.J. & Gilbert, E.E. (2014). VADER: A Parsimonious Rule-based Model for
Sentiment Analysis of Social Media Text. Eighth International Conference on
Weblogs and Social Media (ICWSM-14). Ann Arbor, MI, June 2014.
coding: utf-8
Author: C.J. Hutto
Thanks to George Berry for reducing the time complexity from something like O(N^4) to O(N).
Thanks to Ewan Klein and Pierpaolo Pantone for bringing VADER into NLTK. Those modifications were awesome.
For license information, see LICENSE.TXT

functions.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.negated
documentation: Determine if input contains negation words
#Static methods# #

functions.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.normalize
documentation:
Normalize the score to be between -1 and 1 using an alpha that
approximates the max expected value

functions.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.allcap_differential
documentation:
Check whether just some words in the input are ALL CAPS
:param list words: The words to inspect
:returns: `True` if some but not all items in `words` are ALL CAPS

functions.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.scalar_inc_dec
documentation:
Check if the preceding words increase, decrease, or negate/nullify the
valence

classes.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.SentiText

methods.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.SentiText._strip_punc_if_word
documentation:
Removes all trailing and leading punctuation
If the resulting string has two or fewer characters,
then it was likely an emoticon, so return original string
(ie ":)" stripped would be "", so just return ":)"

methods.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.SentiText._words_and_emoticons
documentation:
Removes leading and trailing puncutation
Leaves contractions and most emoticons
    Does not preserve punc-plus-letter emoticons (e.g. :D)
Identify sentiment-relevant string-level properties of input text.

classes.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.SentimentIntensityAnalyzer

methods.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.SentimentIntensityAnalyzer.make_lex_dict
documentation: Convert lexicon file to a dictionary

methods.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.SentimentIntensityAnalyzer.make_emoji_dict
documentation: Convert emoji lexicon file to a dictionary

methods.name: vaderSentiment.vaderSentiment.SentimentIntensityAnalyzer.polarity_scores
documentation:
Return a float for sentiment strength based on the input text.
Positive values are positive valence, negative value are negative
valence.
Give a sentiment intensity score to sentences.
